title: Giovanni Pico della Mirandola
summary: Prodigy, count, disputant, theologian-philosopher, and unstable emblem of Renaissance dignity. The portal treats Pico as a figure whose short life moved between university scholasticism, Florentine Platonism, Hebrew/Kabbalistic study, papal trouble, and late Savonarolan religion.

title: Astrology Taxonomy
subtitle: Do not tag astrology alone
summary: PicoDB now separates natural celestial influence, judicial astrology, astral magic, poetic astrology, Kabbalistic correspondences, biblical cosmology, anti-astrology, and reception.

title: Christian Kabbalah in Pico
subtitle: Mithridates, Hebrew, 900 Conclusions
summary: Pico's Kabbalah depends on translation, Hebrew aspiration, Christian interpretation, and the ambitious integration of Jewish mystical materials into concordist philosophy.

title: Pico and Astrology
subtitle: From scientia naturalis to Disputationes
summary: Pico's anti-astrology polemic must be studied as a development from earlier natural philosophy and celestial/Kabbalistic interests into late Florentine anti-divinatory critique.

title: Aquinas and Dionysius
subtitle: On Being and Unity
summary: A new essay seed frames On Being and Unity as Pico's Christian metaphysical control of Ficinian-Proclean transcendence through Aquinas and Dionysius.

title: Arabic Intellect Tradition
subtitle: Felicitas and ascent
summary: A new essay seed follows Black's path from Averroes, Avicenna, Al-Farabi, Ibn Bajja, and Maimonides into Pico's Heptaplus and 900 Conclusions.

title: Dionysian Anagogy
subtitle: Hierarchy, symbols, ascent
summary: A new essay seed treats Pseudo-Dionysius as a bridge between Platonism, Christian theology, biblical hermeneutics, and Pico's ascent model.

title: Heptaplus After the Oration
subtitle: Post-trial recoding
summary: A new essay seed reads the Heptaplus as Pico's post-Oration recoding of concord, ascent, Kabbalah, and source density through Genesis.

title: Heptaplus and Hermeneutics
subtitle: Black-guided essay draft
summary: The updated Heptaplus essay reads Genesis as a structured anagogical system involving Aquinas, Dionysius, Arabic intellect theory, Jewish exegesis, Kabbalah, and the 900 Conclusions.

title: Neoplatonists and Arabs
subtitle: Source networks in the 900
summary: A new essay seed maps Proclus, Iamblichus, Pseudo-Dionysius, Averroes, Avicenna, Al-Farabi, and Ibn Bajja as structured source fields in the 900 Conclusions.

title: Pico Biography
subtitle: Scholar-synthesis draft
summary: A new biographical essay treats Pico as a mobile noble scholar whose family privilege, universities, Florentine networks, Kabbalah infrastructure, Roman trial, French detention, and late Savonarolan proximity must be read together.

title: Pico and Astrology
subtitle: From celestial use to anti-divination
summary: A new synthesis essay reads Pico's astrology developmentally: early celestial and poetic uses, Kabbalistic/magical contexts, late critique of divinatory astrology, and complex reception.

title: Pico's Dispute with Ficino
subtitle: Longform essay draft
summary: A new longform essay seed frames the Pico-Ficino dispute through the Commento, On Being and Unity, Oration, 900 Conclusions, and Heptaplus.

title: Pico's Kabbalah
subtitle: Longform scholar synthesis
summary: A new longform essay seed synthesizes Wirszubski, Busi, Copenhaver, Farmer, Howlett, Edelheit, Dougherty, and Allen into a working account of Pico's Kabbalah as source tradition, praxis, apologetics, magic, thesis system, and historiographical problem.

title: Pico's Own-Opinion Theses
subtitle: The 900 as Pico's architecture
summary: A new essay seed starts a table-driven project for all theses secundum opinionem propriam, tracking source family, doctrine, transformation, danger, and cross-links.

title: Plato and Aristotle in Pico
subtitle: Concord as controlled instability
summary: A new essay seed treats Pico's concord as a method of testing, correcting, and hierarchizing Aristotle and Plato rather than as easy harmony.

title: Against the Oration-Only Pico
subtitle: Copenhaver, Farmer, Howlett, Walden
summary: The portal's governing historiographical correction: Pico cannot be reduced to a proto-modern Oration on dignity. The Oration must be read as preface, reception object, mystical text, and part of the 900 Conclusions crisis.

title: Astrology Scholar Matrix
subtitle: Akopyan, Rabin, Rutkin, Vanden Broecke
summary: A new matrix assigns guide roles for Pico's astrology: developmental arc, historiography, early Commento evidence, reform/reception, transmission risk, Ficino relation, and Heptaplus cosmology.

title: Biography Method Matrix
subtitle: How to write Pico's life
summary: The biographical protocol assigns Howlett, Edelheit, Copenhaver, Dougherty/Borghesi, Farmer, Busi, Wirszubski, and Allen distinct roles in controlling life claims.

title: Kabbalah Scholar Matrix
subtitle: Who governs which claims
summary: A new synthesis matrix assigns each major scholar a role in future Kabbalah close readings: source control, praxis, trial danger, thesis clustering, three-pillar concord, scholastic form, corpus discipline, and Platonist boundary control.

title: The Anti-Pico Problem
subtitle: Textual tampering and late doctrine
summary: Farmer's textual-transmission argument warns that late printed Pico, especially the Disputationes, cannot be used doctrinally without checking Gianfrancesco/San Marco risk.

title: Allen's Ficino-Pico Protocol
subtitle: Contentious co-Platonism
summary: Michael J. B. Allen now anchors the portal's treatment of Pico and Ficino: shared Platonism, pointed correction, textual concealment, and technical disputes over One, being, Mind, Soul, Ideas, and the Idea of Man.

title: Brian P. Copenhaver
subtitle: Renaissance philosophy, magic, Pico reception
summary: Copenhaver is a central modern interpreter for this portal because his corpus here ties Pico's Oration to magic, Kabbalah, modern memory, and the scholastic-juridical details of the Roman heresy case.

title: Chaim Wirszubski
subtitle: Pico and Jewish mysticism
summary: Wirszubski clarifies the sources, manuscripts, translations, and Christianizing shape of Pico's Kabbalah, making him essential for any serious portal treatment of Pico's Hebrew and Kabbalistic materials.

title: Copenhaver's Scholarly Values
subtitle: Anti-myth, trial, technical danger
summary: Copenhaver now anchors the portal's resistance to the modern dignity myth and its close attention to the Oration as a whole, the Apology, scholastic Latin, and heresy stakes.

title: Crofton Black
subtitle: Heptaplus and biblical hermeneutics
summary: Black now anchors PicoDB's Heptaplus readings: structure, Genesis, esotericism, Dionysian anagogy, intellect, felicitas, Bereshit, sabbath, and the forty-nine gates.

title: Dougherty's Corpus Standard
subtitle: Genre, editions, collaboration
summary: Dougherty now supplies the corpus-wide discipline for PicoDB: every reading must know its genre, edition status, translation status, reception frame, and specialist needs.

title: Edelheit's Scholastic Trace Method
subtitle: Beyond reductive isms
summary: Edelheit now governs the portal's scholastic-source tagging: Padua, Paris, teachers, school traditions, and the Renaissance scholasticism that keeps Pico from collapsing into a single label.

title: Howlett's Three-Pillar Re-evaluation
subtitle: Aristotle, Plato, Kabbalah
summary: Howlett now shapes readings of Pico as a coherent contested site where Aristotelianism, Platonism, and Jewish Kabbalism converge, succeed, and sometimes expose real differences.

title: Ovanes Akopyan
subtitle: Pico and astrology
summary: Akopyan supplies the main structure for reading Pico's Disputationes, its prehistory, sources, natural philosophy, and pro/contra afterlife.

title: Sophia Howlett
subtitle: Aristotelianism, Kabbalism, Platonism
summary: Howlett re-enters Pico studies from Ficino studies and frames Pico as a contested site whose allure depends on the interplay of exceptionalism, Kabbalah, Aristotelianism, Platonism, and modern scholarly shifts away from an Oration-only Pico.

title: Stephen A. Farmer
subtitle: 900 Theses and syncretic systems
summary: Farmer restores the 900 Conclusions as a massive puzzle and oral-disputational project whose hidden connections structure Pico's Roman ambition.

title: Biography Source Gaps
subtitle: Letters, Vita, witnesses
summary: A new source-gap packet marks the Borghesi Lettere for letter-by-letter summary and identifies Gianfrancesco's Vita, Capponi 235, the 1496 print tradition, PRDL titles, and the del Medigo letter as acquisition/control targets.

title: Astrology Source Packet
subtitle: Six new astrology documents
summary: The pass 010 source packet records Akopyan, Rabin, Rutkin, Vanden Broecke, and review materials newly copied from Downloads and ingested into PicoDB.

title: Black's Heptaplus Structure
subtitle: Seven-part guide to the work
summary: A source packet maps Black's chapter structure and makes it the control frame for future section summaries of Pico's Heptaplus.

title: Busi-Wirszubski Kabbalah Protocol
subtitle: Source control plus praxis
summary: PicoDB now has a Kabbalah protocol for Oration, 900 Conclusions, and Heptaplus readings: Wirszubski governs source precision, while Busi governs praxis, correspondences, and Mithridates' formative mediation.

title: Section Summary Style Guide
subtitle: Arguments, references, coverage
summary: PicoDB now has a formal style guide requiring every section summary to reconstruct argument moves, register references, extract claims, mark uncertainties, and name knowledge-product hooks.

title: Summary Coverage Protocol
subtitle: UNREAD to PROMOTED
summary: The reading system now tracks coverage levels from unread catalog entries through source packets, section summaries, claims, cross-links, review, and website promotion.

title: 900 Conclusions
subtitle: Concordist disputation program
summary: The 900 Conclusions are the structural engine behind Pico's Roman crisis: a massive public program of philosophical and theological theses whose condemned subset produced the trial archive.

title: 900 Conclusions as a Knowledge System
subtitle: Thesis clusters and hidden connections
summary: The 900 Conclusions should be treated as a debate database of compressed propositions, cross-links, traditions, and edition problems.

title: Disputationes adversus astrologiam divinatricem
subtitle: Anti-astrology and late Savonarolan proximity
summary: The anti-astrology work belongs to Pico's late Florentine years and is central for mapping the divergence from Ficino and the shared terrain with Savonarola.

title: Heptaplus
subtitle: Genesis, creation, biblical hermeneutics
summary: Heptaplus shifts Pico away from a simplified secular dignity myth and toward biblical exegesis, creation theology, and the problem of posthumous textual transmission.

title: Heptaplus: Genesis and the Four Worlds
subtitle: Sevenfold Mosaic interpretation
summary: The Heptaplus reads Genesis as hidden natural and divine philosophy, organized by coherent sevenfold interpretation and a four-world cosmology.

title: On Being and Unity: Ficino Controversy
subtitle: Parmenides, Sophist, One
summary: The On Being and Unity dossier now treats the work as both concordist metaphysics and a polemical correction of Ficino's Proclean Plato.

title: On Being and the One
subtitle: Concord and apophasis
summary: Pico reconciles Plato and Aristotle by distinguishing senses of being and by moving from semantics to Dionysian divine darkness.

title: Oration on the Dignity of Man
subtitle: Roman disputation preface turned modern emblem
summary: The Oration must be treated both as a text attached to the failed 900 Theses debate and as a later object of modern memory, humanist canonization, and debate over dignity, freedom, magic, and Kabbalah.

title: Oration: Indeterminacy and Ascent
subtitle: Opening sequence
summary: The Oration's famous opening is a disciplined program of self-shaping, angelic imitation, philosophical purgation, and ascent to union with God.

title: Oration: Indeterminate Adam
subtitle: Philebus and Idea of Man
summary: Allen's metaphysical reread turns the Oration opening from a modern freedom slogan into a Platonic problem of potentiality, form, whole-in-part logic, and Christological anthropology.
